title: Fresh-tyre wave among peers
summary: Describe whether other cars on the same lap have recently switched to fresh tyres or new slick compounds.
rationale: A driver may respond to competitors entering a new stint. Run variants with pit-wave and fresh-tyre peer features were plausible, though exact implementations varied.
implementation_hint: Within race-lap groups, compute leave-one-out rates of peers with very low `TyreLife`, current slick newly observed, or current `PitStop` active. Add own pressure times peer fresh-rate interactions.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with low-medium confidence.
risk: Requires chronological new-slick flags to be computed safely before same-lap aggregation; avoid future compound history.
